home.name: Benoit Tremblay
home.heading: Building things that matter.
home.sub: Computer science student driven by curiosity and a love for creating. Discover my background, explore my projects, and feel free to reach out if you'd like to work together.
home.techDesc: Full-stack websites, desktop apps, mobile apps, and games.
home.aboutDesc: A little about who I am, my background, and the tools I work with.
home.contactDesc: Open to opportunities, collabs, and questions.

web_apps.stack: Laravel · ASP.NET · REST · JSP · TypeScript · Next.js
web_apps.detail: Full-stack apps from idea to deployment. Clean architecture, proper auth, CI/CD pipelines.

apis.stack: Java · Kotlin · React Native · Android · iOS
apis.detail: Native and cross-platform mobile apps. From Android to iOS, built for performance and real device feel.

desktop_apps.stack: JavaScript · Python · Electron · Next.js · Qt · Cross-platform
desktop_apps.detail: Desktop applications built with Electron and Python. Packaged as standalone executables for Windows, macOS and Linux.

game_dev.detail: Games and interactive experiences from prototype to polished release.

bio.p1: I started gymnastics at age <highlight>3</highlight>, drawn to the combination of strength, precision, and artistry the sport demands. Over the years I've competed across multiple countries and learned that the mental discipline required in the gym translates directly to every other area of life.
bio.p2: My interest in <highlight>computer science</highlight> comes from wanting to understand the systems and logic behind the tools we use every day. I started with small scripts and have since moved into building full-stack applications with real users in mind.
bio.p3: I'm not someone who does things halfway. <highlight>Practice, feedback, and growth</highlight> drive everything I do. I give 100% to every project, always with the goal of making the end experience the best it can possibly be for the people using it.

projectsBuilt.title: Projects Built
projectsBuilt.body: Most of my projects — full-stack apps, REST APIs, desktop applications, and games — built with modern technologies and real users in mind.

repos.title: Projects & Repositories
repos.body: Public repositories on GitHub — ranging from full-stack web apps to desktop applications and personal experiments. Each one is a snapshot of something I was learning or building at the time.

topLang.title: Top Languages
topLang.body: Languages I use most across my public repositories.

skills.heading: Technical skills
skills.legend: Technologies, frameworks and tools I've learned, worked with and use in projects.

projects.pageSubtitle: A selection of things I've built — full-stack apps, games, websites, and experiments.

studentResidence.title: Student Residence
studentResidence.description: A web application built with ASP.NET Core MVC to manage student residences, housing applications, and user accounts — with three access levels: student, manager, and administrator.

conferenceManager.title: Workshop Manager
conferenceManager.description: A Laravel web application for scheduling and managing workshops during the Humanities Week event — handling facilitators, rooms, and scheduling conflicts, with a public-facing schedule and a participant registration system.

fileManagers.title: File Managers
fileManagers.description: A JSP web application built with Spring Boot and a JWT-secured REST API — featuring user management, file uploading, and a comment system with moderation.

portfolio.title: My Portfolio
portfolio.description: A personal portfolio website built with Next.js and TypeScript — showcasing my projects and skills, with multilingual support and dark/light mode.

olympicWave.title: Olympic Wave
olympicWave.description: A marketing website for a sports coaching service, built with React and deployed on Netlify.

lanRadar.title: Lan Radar
lanRadar.description: A desktop application built with Next.js and Electron to monitor your local network — see connected devices, ping them in real time, run speed tests, and browse installed applications on your machine.

lolDashboard.title: LoL Client Dashboard
lolDashboard.description: An open-source desktop application built with Next.js and Electron to interact with the League of Legends client via the LCU API — manage lobbies, automate champion select, auto-accept matches, and invite friends, all from a single interface.

theLastWait.title: The Last Wait
theLastWait.description: A solo-developed psychological horror game with a deep, narrative-driven story, set in the fading mind of an elderly man confined to a hospital bed, where reality and nightmare blur as he waits for his daughter. Features immersive audio design.

zombieGame.title: Zombie Game
zombieGame.description: A solo-developed round-based zombie survival shooter inspired by COD Zombies, with increasing difficulty, a boss, multiple weapons, and light RPG-style stat upgrades. Still a work in progress with known bugs.

magicianBrowser.title: Magician Adventure (Browser)
magicianBrowser.description: A browser-based RPG adventure game built with PHP and JavaScript.

teamManager.title: Team Manager For A Store
teamManager.description: An Android app to manage store employees and departments.

remindersPlanner.title: Reminders Planner
remindersPlanner.description: A cross-platform mobile app to create, edit, and receive notifications for personal reminders.

taskManager.title: Client/Server Task Manager
taskManager.description: A Java client-server app for managing tasks across a network connection.

magicianConsole.title: Magician Adventure (Console)
magicianConsole.description: A turn-based console RPG built in C# — battle enemies, unlock stronger spells, and see how long you can survive.

vehiculeLocation.title: Vehicule Location
vehiculeLocation.description: A desktop app for managing vehicle rentals, with separate client and employee views.

contact.pageSubtitle: Have a project in mind, a question, or just want to say hello? Fill out the form and I'll get back to you as soon as possible.
